creator: junchoon@dec.sakura.ne.jp
text:
(In reply to Michael from comment #16)

What confumed me is:

> "now" (since dec-21) is out the nvidia-driver-390-147 which supports newer linux kernels and should also address the FreeBSD problem in this sense,

in your first post.

And

> seems what you're looking for is a reason not to do what you should do,

With this regard, nothing I should do. Not at all at least for me.

I'm 100% a volunteer within my spare times, no sponsors, no authorities to access to nvidia confidential resources. And dropping old GPU supports are on decisions by nvidia, not FreeBSD side.

And I'm no longer using legacy drivers myself. Don't have enough time to dive into Linux world and hardware to test legacy drivers. And unfortunately, in some cases, problems could happen on specific hardware only.
What you're saying here clearly means that I should purchase hardwares to test with my personal budgets and keep enough time (regardless $work and health issues) to investigate. It's simply impossible for me.

Why I'm working on x11/nvidia-driver is just because the latest versions still supports my GPU in hand. Why I'm submitting patches to update legacy drivers is just because usually it's trivial (for allowing build and package) to do so, compared with supporting newer versions in some cases like 570 series.

Don't forget FreeBSD is one of the free-of-charge OSS and most of developers/porters/testers are volunteers. What can be done is quite limited, but doing their/my best "possible".


> the xf86-nv drv isn't working long time, I never tried again, I believe nouveau also not, whatsoever, having the driver loaded at boot is necessary for servers to get a proper console font resolution

AFAIK, nouveau driver is NOT ported to FreeBSD (no person popped in to make it work on FreeBSD and it would be non-trivial).
And IIUC, xf86-video-nv is updated just because it still builds on FreeBSD.
Not sure anyone is testing it whether it's working or not.
